notes:
The game crashes during the first splash screen.
Backtrace tells about exception during  _FindAndUnlinkFrame in msvcr110
title: City Car Driving

notes:
Both career and free drive modes have been tested for several hours each, and it appears to be generally up and running using default proton, and certainly works to a perfectly playable level.

I've used it with keyboard and with a Logotech G920, which is mostly working fine. The pedals needed remapping in the controls section, but otherwise work properly. Keyboard remapping is not detecting my UK Laptop keyboard layout, so when it asks for "\" I have to press "#", for example, but otherwise works properly.

Force Feedback is inconstent i.e. when you start driving it's sometimes there and sometimes not - then if you retry a lesson, or return to menu, it might be working/not working next time. Audio and radio is working, though I've not so far managed to get any custom radio stations to work. No graphical issues seen on default settings.

Note that before running it, I have to run "sudo vulkaninfo" in terminal, as Vulkan is not initialising correctly without this (I think this is a general issue, rather than specific to this game).

There are occasional in game crashes, though I believe they're part of the original program and not specifically Proton issues. I've not tested multiple monitor setups.
title: City Car Driving

notes:
The game refuses to run at all on any Proton version other than 4.2-2. Once running you're greeted with horrible framerate issues and crashes more frequent than what I experienced with Windows 10. Framerates range from 10-100+ and is constantly bouncing. Game refuses to launch at all with d3d11/wined3d at all and Esync off makes no difference.

Best I achieved before giving up was disabling multi-threading in the settings within the game. But performance still isn't anything to scream about as you'd imagine. Give it a pass, save yourself some time.
title: City Car Driving

notes: Platinum because the game MIGHT run perfectly out of the box (as was the case with my wife's PC), but in my case buttons and a few tooltips appeared in russian instead of english. To fix that, in properties, launch options, use "LANGUAGE=en_GB.UTF-8 TZ= %command%", for some reason en_US does not work. Or use your own national language. It will also get the language settings from the Steam language configuration for most strings. Also, if you have a G29, clutch, brake and gas might be out of order, so you have to reconfigure, other buttons are correct. If you have a G27 (as my wife has), you have to reconfigure not only clutch/brake/gas but almost all buttons and even the H-shifter. Apart from that, the game runs smoothly and we and my wife had no crashes or issues after several hours playing.
title: City Car Driving

notes:
To run in English, set Launch Options to:  "LANGUAGE=en_GB.UTF-8 %command%"
Make sure that English United Kingdom UTF-8 is fully installed in your system's Language Settings (initially I did not and it corrupted my game install). Game runs fine on my system with high graphics settings. Play tested free driving for an hour.
title: City Car Driving

notes: Game simply won't start at all. Tried no d3d11 and wined3d11 with no success. Others with nvidia GPUs seems to have some success, so might be because I have AMD, but hard to tell.
title: City Car Driving

notes: All fine in all modes, after approx 20 hours of play. Working with Logitech G920, with occasional Force Feedback (It is sometimes on or off after returning to menu). G920 pedals needed remapping, but fine afterwards. Radio and custom stations all fine (navigate to the compatdata folder of the game, then through to Steam user's My Documents folder to edit radio.ini).
title: City Car Driving
